name: String axiverse
summary: String compactifications generically produce hundreds of axion-like fields, populating each decade of mass from ~10^-33 eV (the Hubble scale today) up to the QCD axion's natural window. Multiple distinct ALPs arise from the topology of the extra-dimensional manifold; their masses, couplings, and abundances are calculable from the compactification data. Predicts simultaneous experimental signatures across CMB rotation, black-hole superradiance, structure formation, and direct detection.

addresses_puzzles.note: Includes a QCD axion among the many ALPs in the spectrum, but the QCD-axion mass is not specially favored by the landscape; finding it requires that one of the many ALPs lie in the 10^-12 - 10^-3 eV window.

Ultralight ALPs in the 10^-22 - 10^-18 eV range (or QCD-axion-mass range) can be cold dark matter via misalignment; the multiplicity of the axiverse populates several DM candidates per compactification.

touches_benchmarks.note: QCD axion within the ADMX-sensitive window if one of the axiverse ALPs lies in the 1-100 mu eV range; haloscope searches directly target the prediction.
Ultralight DM with m ~ 10^-22 eV suppresses small-scale power; Lyman-alpha bounds (Iršič et al. 2017) push m > 2e-21 eV, constraining axiverse DM in the fuzzy regime.

ALPs with m ~ 10^-33 - 10^-28 eV give a uniform rotation of the CMB polarization (cosmic birefringence) at the 0.3 deg level; recent claimed detection by Minami-Komatsu (2020) is plausibly consistent.

new_scales: each ALP has its own decay constant f_i ~ M_GUT and mass m_i exponentially sensitive to topological data
introduces.note: Compactification topology, string scale, and a few coarse parameters control the spectrum; individual ALP masses are exponentially small and effectively scanned.

history: Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell (2009), 'String Axiverse', Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530, arXiv:0905.4720. Sparked active black-hole-superradiance, fuzzy-DM, cosmic-birefringence, and ALP-haloscope research programs.

references.citation: Arvanitaki, Dimopoulos, Dubovsky, Kaloper, March-Russell, 'String Axiverse', Phys. Rev. D 81, 123530 (2010)
